title: bigelowia douglasii var. puberula
intro: Bigelowia douglasii var. puberula, also known as Douglas' goldeneyes or Bigelow's goldeneyes, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, thanks to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Bigelowia douglasii var. puberula include its potential to soothe sore throats, calm bronchitis, and alleviate sinus infections. Bigelowia douglasii var. puberula can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves, flowers, and roots are used in herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
